items.title: investingLive Americas FX news wrap 2 Jun: Markets randomly walk through the flow of news
items.summary: Bitcoin has lost its cool Copper prices are nearing records as analysts get increasingly bullish Trump to Iran: It is time to make a deal Crude oil prices move above the 200 hour MA at $92.48 Hezbollah will not accept partial ceasefire with Israel - report Bank of England's Greene: Risk of acting is less severe than the risk of failing to act Rubio: We are in talks with Iran BOE's Bailey: The inflation overshoot is entirely due to events in the Persian Gulf US April JOLTS job openings 7.618m vs 6.88m expected First they came for the free cash flow, then the came for the equity Fed's Hammack: We may need to act soon if inflation trends don't cool USD is mixed to kickstart NA trading on June 2. What are the technicals telling traders? investingLive European FX news wrap: Optimism continues to prevail in the markets ECB policymaker Rehn says June rate move would be an "insurance" hike Marvell Technology stock soars as Nvidia CEO says it's "the next trillion-dollar company" The April JOLTS report was stronger than expected, with job openings rising sharply to 7.62 million, the highest level since May 2024 and well above forecasts. The increase suggests labor demand remains resilient despite concerns about slowing economic growth. While hiring eased somewhat, job openings and quits both moved higher, indicating workers remain confident about employment opportunities. Overall, the report provides evidence that the labor market remains relatively healthy and may reduce some of the pressure on the Fed to consider near-term rate cuts, although one month's data is not enough to signal a broader shift in the labor market trend. ECB policymaker Olli Rehn suggested that a June rate hike should be viewed as an "insurance" move designed to guard against potential future inflation risks rather than the start of a sustained tightening cycle. Rehn emphasized that inflation expectations remain well anchored, but argued that a modest policy adjustment would help ensure inflation stays under control amid heightened uncertainty. His comments imply that the ECB is likely to present any June hike as a precautionary measure rather than a signal of multiple rate increases ahead. As a result, the market may be overestimating the chances of a follow-up hike in July, with policymakers more likely to pause and assess incoming economic data and geopolitical developments, including the evolving US-Iran situation, before considering any further action later in the year. Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack reinforced her hawkish stance, arguing that it is reasonable to keep interest rates unchanged for now given ongoing uncertainties, but warning that waiting too long could allow inflation pressures to become more deeply embedded in the economy. She said her primary concern is the growing risk of persistent inflation, noting that monetary policy may not be restrictive enough to return inflation to the Fed's 2% target. Hammack emphasized that inflationary pressures are broadening across the economy, while the labor market remains near full employment. As a result, she remains firmly committed to restoring price stability and signaled that policymakers should remain vigilant against the risk that inflation becomes entrenched, requiring tighter policy for longer. Major US stock indices closed higher with the Dow industrial average leading the way with a gain of 0.45%. The S&P index is up 0.13% and the NASDAQ index was unchanged. Despite the rise in the Dow, 14 of the 30 stocks were higher while 16 or lower. The Dow's biggest winners today were led by Cisco, which surged 5.50%, followed closely by Caterpillar, up 5.16%. The strong gains in those two stocks helped drive broader strength within the index. Apple added 2.91%, continuing to benefit from investor interest in large-cap technology shares, while IBM climbed 2.75% on solid buying interest. Goldman Sachs rounded out the top performers, rising 1.53%. The Dow's biggest losers were led by Nike, which fell 4.79%, making it the weakest performer in the index. Microsoft and Salesforce each declined 4.18%, weighing on the technology sector despite strength elsewhere in the market. Boeing dropped 2.94%, giving back earlier gains and adding pressure to the industrial segment, while Amazon slipped 1.83% as investors took profits in the e-commerce and cloud-computing giant. The losses were concentrated in consumer discretionary and technology names, with Nike, Microsoft, and Salesforce accounting for the bulk of the downside among Dow components. Alphabet shares fell 3.86% after the company announced an $80 billion equity raise, a move that will dilute existing shareholders by roughly 1.7% and highlights the enormous costs associated with the AI arms race. Over the past year, major technology companies have shifted from generating substantial free cash flow to spending aggressively on AI chips, talent, and data centers, followed by large debt issuances. After already raising about $85 billion in debt, Alphabet is now turning to equity markets, with the offering including $10 billion sold directly to Berkshire Hathaway, approximately $30 billion in public offerings, and up to $40 billion through an at-the-market share issuance program. The move is being viewed as a watershed moment, signaling that AI investment demands are becoming so large that even the biggest tech firms are seeking new sources of capital, while also increasing competition for investor dollars ahead of expected IPOs from companies such as SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI. The fall was felt by a number of large cap names with: Nvidia: -0.69% Amazon -1.81% Microsoft -4.17% Other losers included: Shake Shack -8.37% to its lowest level going back to November 2023 Dell fell -6.54% as traders replaced Dell which was up 300% from the February low, with Hewlett-Packard Enterprise (up 19.47% after its earnings beat). Strategy (Microstrategy) tumbled by -9.15% as Bitcoin tumbled by -5.7% Figma fell by -10.53%. Remember when it went public in July 2025 trading as high as $142.92. In reached a low in April at $16.60 before rebounding recently to $27.74. Today's decline took the price to $24.29 at the close Palantir-5.28% What did not go down and helped to balance the overall indices: Marvell surged by 32.52% after Jenson Huang of Nvidia touted the company as a future $1T company. The capitalization is abour $200-$250M. Hewlett-Packard enterprise rose 19.47% after earnings Cisco rose by 5.5% Apple rose by 2.90% IBM rose by 2.75% US yields are marginally laureth the front-end with the two-year down -0.4 basis points at 4.047%. The 10 year fell by -2.4 basis points to 4.453% while the 30 year fell by -2.6 basis points at 4.965%. Looking at the US dollar, it was mixed with the EUR, CHF and CAD within 0.05% of the closing level from yesterday. The USD was the strongest vs the JPY at 0.18% and weakest vs the AUD at -0.36%. The USDJPY moved to a high of $159.98, just short of the 160.00 level that traders are worried about with regard to intervention threats. Overall the USD index (DXY) is ending the day near unchanged. Bitcoin is currently down $-4300 or -6.01% at $67,033. The low price reached $66,303. The high price was at $71,310. The close today is the lowest since early April. The low close for the year was in February at $62,795. The high for the year is at $97,939 (from January 14). Crude oil is trading up $1.22 at $93.40. The high price reached $94 while the low price was at $90.12. Progress on the Middle East continues to remain muddied and full of unsubstantiated rumors mostly. Israeli and Lebanon continue to add to the headwinds for the opening of the Straits of Hormuz and a cease-fire. What was interesting is that prospects for resumption of peace talks on Ukraine are linked to the end of the conflict around Iran (according to Tass at least). This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 比特币热度消退 铜价逼近历史高位，分析师看涨情绪日益高涨 特朗普对伊朗表示：是时候达成协议了 原油价格突破200小时均线，报92.48美元 报道称真主党不会接受与以色列的部分停火 英格兰银行的格林表示：采取行动的风险小于不采取行动的风险 卢比奥表示：我们正在与伊朗进行谈判 英格兰银行的贝利表示： 通胀超调完全归因于波斯湾局势 美国4月JOLTS职位空缺761.8万，远超预期688万 先是针对自由现金流，随后转向股权 美联储哈马克：若通胀趋势不降温，我们可能需要尽快采取行动 6月2日北美交易时段开盘，美元涨跌互现。技术指标向交易员传递了什么信号？investingLive欧洲外汇新闻综述： 市场乐观情绪持续主导 欧洲央行决策者雷恩表示6月加息将作为“保险”措施 英伟达CEO称Marvell Technology是“下一个万亿市值公司”，其股价飙升 4月JOLTS报告强于预期，职位空缺数激增至762万，创2024年5月以来新高，远超市场预期。 这一增长表明，尽管存在经济增长放缓的担忧，但劳动力需求依然坚挺。虽然招聘活动有所放缓，但职位空缺和离职人数均有所增加，表明劳动者对就业机会仍持乐观态度。总体而言，该报告提供了劳动力市场依然相对健康的证据，这可能减轻美联储考虑短期内降息的部分压力，尽管单月数据尚不足以表明劳动力市场趋势发生了更广泛的转变。欧洲央行决策者奥利·雷恩表示，6月的加息应被视为一项旨在防范未来潜在通胀风险的“保险”举措，而非持续紧缩周期的开端。雷恩强调，通胀预期依然稳固，但他认为，在不确定性加剧的情况下，适度的政策调整将有助于确保通胀保持在可控范围内。他的言论暗示，欧洲央行可能会将6月的任何加息视为一项预防性措施，而非未来多次加息的信号。 因此，市场可能高估了7月再次加息的可能性，政策制定者更可能暂停行动，先评估即将公布的经济数据和地缘政治局势（包括美伊局势的发展），然后再考虑今年晚些时候采取进一步行动。克利夫兰联储主席贝丝·哈马克重申了其鹰派立场，她认为鉴于当前的不确定性，暂时维持利率不变是合理的，但警告称等待时间过长可能会导致通胀压力在经济中根深蒂固。她表示，其主要担忧是通胀持续的风险日益增加，并指出货币政策可能不够紧缩，无法将通胀率降回美联储2%的目标水平。哈马克强调，通胀压力正在经济各领域蔓延，而劳动力市场仍接近充分就业。因此，她坚定致力于恢复物价稳定，并暗示决策者应保持警惕，防范通胀根深蒂固的风险，这可能需要更长时间维持更紧缩的政策。 美国主要股指收高，道琼斯工业平均指数领涨，上涨0.45%。标普500指数上涨0.13%，纳斯达克指数收平。尽管道指整体上涨，但30只成分股中仅有14只上涨，16只下跌。今日道指涨幅最大的个股由思科领衔，该股大涨5.50%，紧随其后的是卡特彼勒，上涨5.16%。这两只股票的强劲涨幅助推了指数整体走强。苹果上涨2.91%，继续受益于投资者对大型科技股的兴趣；IBM在强劲买盘推动下上涨2.75%。高盛以1.53%的涨幅位列表现最佳股之列。道指跌幅最大的是耐克，下跌4.79%，成为指数中表现最差的个股。微软和Salesforce均下跌4.18%，尽管市场其他板块表现强劲，但仍拖累了科技板块。波音下跌2.94%，回吐了早盘涨幅，给工业板块带来压力；而亚马逊下跌1.83%，因投资者对这家电商和云计算巨头获利了结。 跌幅主要集中在可选消费品和科技类股，道指成分股中耐克、微软和Salesforce的跌幅最大。Alphabet宣布将进行800亿美元的增资后，其股价下跌3.86%。此举将导致现有股东持股比例稀释约1.7%，也凸显了人工智能军备竞赛带来的巨大成本。过去一年间，各大科技公司已从产生大量自由现金流转向在AI芯片、人才和数据中心方面进行激进投资，随后又进行了大规模的债务发行。在已通过债务融资筹集约850亿美元后，Alphabet现正转向股权市场，此次融资包括直接向伯克希尔·哈撒韦出售100亿美元股份、约300亿美元的公开发行，以及通过“按市价发行”计划筹集的最高400亿美元。此举被视为一个分水岭时刻，表明人工智能投资需求已变得如此庞大，以至于连最大的科技公司都在寻求新的资本来源，同时在SpaceX、Anthropic和OpenAI等公司预计进行首次公开募股（IPO）之前，投资者资金的争夺也日益激烈。 多家大盘股受到此次下跌影响，包括：英伟达：-0.69% 亚马逊：-1.81% 微软：-4.17% 其他下跌个股还包括： Shake Shack下跌8.37%，创下自2023年11月以来的最低水平；戴尔下跌6.54%，因交易员抛售了自2月低点以来已上涨300%的戴尔，转而买入惠普企业（其财报超预期后上涨19.47%）。微策略（MicroStrategy）暴跌9.15%，因比特币暴跌5.7%；Figma下跌10.53%。还记得它2025年7月上市时曾一度涨至142.92美元吗？该股4月曾触及16.60美元低点，近期反弹至27.74美元。今日下跌后，收盘价回落至24.29美元。Palantir下跌5.28%。未随大盘下跌并助力平衡整体指数的个股：Marvell大涨32.52%，此前英伟达的黄仁勋盛赞该公司未来有望成为市值1万亿美元的企业。该公司市值约为2亿至2.5亿美元。惠普企业财报发布后上涨19.47%，思科上涨5.5%，苹果上涨2.90%，IBM上涨2.75%。美国国债收益率在短期端小幅走低，两年期收益率下跌0.4个基点至4.047%。10年期国债收益率下跌2.4个基点至4.453%，30年期国债收益率下跌2.6个基点至4.965%。 从美元走势来看，其对欧元、瑞士法郎和加元的汇率涨跌互现，波动幅度均在昨日收盘价的0.05%以内。美元对日元表现最强，上涨0.18%；对澳元表现最弱，下跌0.36%。美元兑日元一度升至159.98美元的高位，略低于160.00这一令交易员担忧可能引发干预的关口。总体而言，美元指数（DXY）今日收盘基本持平。比特币目前下跌4300美元，跌幅6.01%，报67,033美元。盘中低点触及66,303美元，高点为71,310美元。今日收盘价为4月初以来最低。今年最低收盘价出现在2月，为62,795美元。年内最高价为97,939美元（1月14日创下）。原油价格上涨1.22美元，报93.40美元。盘中最高触及94美元，最低为90.12美元。中东局势进展依然扑朔迷离，且充斥着大量未经证实的传闻。以色列与黎巴嫩局势持续为霍尔木兹海峡的开放及停火协议增添阻力。值得注意的是，乌克兰和平谈判重启的前景似乎与伊朗周边冲突的结束息息相关（至少据塔斯社报道如此）。 本文由investinglive.com的格雷格·米哈洛夫斯基撰写。
items.summaryEn: Bitcoin has lost its cool Copper prices are nearing records as analysts get increasingly bullish Trump to Iran: It is time to make a deal Crude oil prices move above the 200 hour MA at $92.48 Hezbollah will not accept partial ceasefire with Israel - report Bank of England's Greene: Risk of acting is less severe than the risk of failing to act Rubio: We are in talks with Iran BOE's Bailey: The inflation overshoot is entirely due to events in the Persian Gulf US April JOLTS job openings 7.618m vs 6.88m expected First they came for the free cash flow, then the came for the equity Fed's Hammack: We may need to act soon if inflation trends don't cool USD is mixed to kickstart NA trading on June 2. What are the technicals telling traders? investingLive European FX news wrap: Optimism continues to prevail in the markets ECB policymaker Rehn says June rate move would be an "insurance" hike Marvell Technology stock soars as Nvidia CEO says it's "the next trillion-dollar company" The April JOLTS report was stronger than expected, with job openings rising sharply to 7.62 million, the highest level since May 2024 and well above forecasts. The increase suggests labor demand remains resilient despite concerns about slowing economic growth. While hiring eased somewhat, job openings and quits both moved higher, indicating workers remain confident about employment opportunities. Overall, the report provides evidence that the labor market remains relatively healthy and may reduce some of the pressure on the Fed to consider near-term rate cuts, although one month's data is not enough to signal a broader shift in the labor market trend. ECB policymaker Olli Rehn suggested that a June rate hike should be viewed as an "insurance" move designed to guard against potential future inflation risks rather than the start of a sustained tightening cycle. Rehn emphasized that inflation expectations remain well anchored, but argued that a modest policy adjustment would help ensure inflation stays under control amid heightened uncertainty. His comments imply that the ECB is likely to present any June hike as a precautionary measure rather than a signal of multiple rate increases ahead. As a result, the market may be overestimating the chances of a follow-up hike in July, with policymakers more likely to pause and assess incoming economic data and geopolitical developments, including the evolving US-Iran situation, before considering any further action later in the year. Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack reinforced her hawkish stance, arguing that it is reasonable to keep interest rates unchanged for now given ongoing uncertainties, but warning that waiting too long could allow inflation pressures to become more deeply embedded in the economy. She said her primary concern is the growing risk of persistent inflation, noting that monetary policy may not be restrictive enough to return inflation to the Fed's 2% target. Hammack emphasized that inflationary pressures are broadening across the economy, while the labor market remains near full employment. As a result, she remains firmly committed to restoring price stability and signaled that policymakers should remain vigilant against the risk that inflation becomes entrenched, requiring tighter policy for longer. Major US stock indices closed higher with the Dow industrial average leading the way with a gain of 0.45%. The S&P index is up 0.13% and the NASDAQ index was unchanged. The strong gains in those two stocks helped drive broader strength within the index. Apple added 2.91%, continuing to benefit from investor interest in large-cap technology shares, while IBM climbed 2.75% on solid buying interest. Goldman Sachs rounded out the top performers, rising 1.53%. The Dow's biggest losers were led by Nike, which fell 4.79%, making it the weakest performer in the index. Microsoft and Salesforce each declined 4.18%, weighing on the technology sector despite strength elsewhere in the market. After already raising about $85 billion in debt, Alphabet is now turning to equity markets, with the offering including $10 billion sold directly to Berkshire Hathaway, approximately $30 billion in public offerings, and up to $40 billion through an at-the-market share issuance program. The move is being viewed as a watershed moment, signaling that AI investment demands are becoming so large that even the biggest tech firms are seeking new sources of capital, while also increasing competition for investor dollars ahead of expected IPOs from companies such as SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI. items.title: investingLive European FX news wrap: Optimism continues to prevail in the markets
items.summary: ECB policymaker Rehn says June rate move would be an "insurance" hike Marvell Technology stock soars as Nvidia CEO says it's "the next trillion-dollar company" Euro area inflation continues to pick up in May, core prices nudge up as well Gold analysis shows it repaired higher. See my targets. UK mortgage approvals climbs in April, holds above six-month average European Parliament votes to remove import duties on US goods to comply with trade deal BoJ taper debate heats up as rising yields complicate exit strategy The Japanese yen extends losses as June rate hike bets continue to look wrong Palladium is looking good for the bulls. See where PA can go & where that might change Gold nudges back up today but still caught in a bit of a technical bind What are the main events for today? Iran reportedly still discussing final text of agreement, no response sent to US yet FX option expiries for 2 June 10am New York cut OpenAI Florida Lawsuit: What AI Stock Investors Should Watch Next Markets continue to stay on edge awaiting US-Iran deal Crude oil analysis today: oil futures fail near 94.20 resistance, testing lower value It's been a pretty calm session with limited data and news releases. The main highlight of the session was the release of the Eurozone Flash CPI report for May. The headline inflation rate rose to 3.2% Y/Y, matching expectations and accelerating from 3.0% in April. The increase was driven mainly by higher energy prices, while underlying inflation pressures also strengthened as Core CPI Y/Y climbed to 2.5% from 2.2% in the prior month. The data reinforced expectations that the ECB is going to raise interest rates at its June meeting but didn't change much in terms of total 2026 pricing. ECB policymaker Rehn confirmed that an interest rate increase at the June meeting should be viewed as an "insurance" move to guard against future inflation risks, even if current inflation expectations remain well anchored. The characterization of a June rate move as an "insurance hike" indicates that policymakers are unlikely to deliver a back-to-back rate hike in July as some analysts have been suggesting without a strong signal from the data. The ECB is most likely to frame the move as an insurance and then wait at least until September to see how the data and the US-Iran situation evolves over the summer. In terms of US-Iran news, Iran is reportedly still reviewing and discussing the final text of a proposed agreement with the United States and has not yet submitted an official response to Washington. While negotiations appear to be progressing, Tehran is seeking amendments and additional assurances before signing off, underscoring that significant details remain unresolved and that a final deal may not be as close as previously expected. Trump said that a deal could happen over the next week but we've heard this so many times before already. In the markets, optimism continues to prevail despite the extended US-Iran stalemate. Markets freaked out for a moment yesterday when Tasnim reported that Iran would stop message exchange with the US in protest against Israeli strikes on Lebanon but things got back to normal very quickly once Trump announced on Truth Social that there would be no troops going to Beirut, and that he had a very good call with Hezbollah which agreed that all shooting will stop. In the American session, the only highlight is the US Job Openings data for April. Job Openings are expected at 6.880M vs 8.866M prior. The data won't change anything for the Fed as all the more timely US jobs data has been pointing to a resilient/strengthening labour market. The market is pricing 15 bps of tightening for the Fed by year-end, with 47% chance of a rate hike in December. This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 欧洲央行决策者雷恩表示，6月的加息将是一次“保险性”加息 英伟达CEO称Marvell Technology是“下一个万亿市值公司”，其股价飙升 5月欧元区通胀持续攀升，核心通胀也小幅上升 黄金分析显示其已回升至更高水平。请参阅我的目标价。英国4月抵押贷款批准量攀升，维持在六个月均值之上。欧洲议会投票决定取消对美国商品的进口关税，以遵守贸易协议。随着收益率上升使退出策略复杂化，日本央行缩减购债规模的讨论愈演愈烈。随着6月加息预期持续被证明是错误的，日元延续跌势。钯金对多头而言前景看好。 看看PA可能走向何方，以及这可能带来哪些变化 黄金今日小幅回升，但仍陷于技术面困境 今天有哪些主要事件？据报道，伊朗仍在商讨协议最终文本，尚未向美国发出回复 6月2日纽约时间上午10点外汇期权到期 OpenAI佛罗里达州诉讼案：AI股票投资者接下来应关注什么 市场持续保持警惕，等待美伊协议 今日原油分析：原油期货在94.20阻力位附近受阻，正测试更低价位 由于数据和新闻发布有限，今日交易时段整体较为平静。本交易日的主要亮点是欧元区5月初值CPI报告的发布。整体通胀率同比升至3.2%，符合预期，且较4月的3.0%有所加快。此次涨幅主要受能源价格上涨推动，同时核心通胀压力也有所增强，核心CPI同比增速从上月的2.2%升至2.5%。该数据强化了市场对欧洲央行将在6月会议上加息的预期，但对2026年整体利率定价影响有限。 欧洲央行决策者雷恩确认，即使当前通胀预期保持稳定，6月会议上的加息也应被视为防范未来通胀风险的“保险”举措。将6月的加息定性为“保险性加息”表明，在缺乏数据发出的强烈信号的情况下，决策者不太可能像一些分析师所建议的那样在7月连续加息。欧洲央行最可能将此次加息定性为“保险性”举措，随后至少等到9月，以观察夏季期间数据及美伊局势的演变。关于美伊局势，据报道，伊朗仍在审议和讨论与美国拟议协议的最终文本，尚未向华盛顿提交正式答复。尽管谈判看似在推进，但德黑兰方面在签署前仍寻求修改条款并获得额外保证，这凸显出诸多关键细节尚未解决，最终协议的达成可能并不像此前预期的那样近在咫尺。特朗普表示协议可能在未来一周内达成，但我们此前已听过太多类似的说法。 尽管美伊僵局持续，市场仍保持乐观情绪。昨日，塔斯尼姆通讯社报道称伊朗将停止与美国的信息交流，以抗议以色列对黎巴嫩的空袭，市场一度陷入恐慌；但特朗普在Truth Social上宣布不会向贝鲁特派遣军队，并表示他与真主党进行了一次非常好的通话，双方同意停止一切交火后，市场很快恢复平静。在美股交易时段，唯一值得关注的焦点是4月份的美国职位空缺数据。市场预期职位空缺数为688万，而前值为886.6万。该数据不会改变美联储的政策走向，因为所有更及时的美国就业数据均显示劳动力市场韧性十足且持续走强。市场目前预期美联储年底前将加息15个基点，12月加息的概率为47%。本文作者为investinglive.com的Giuseppe Dellamotta。
items.summaryEn: ECB policymaker Rehn says June rate move would be an "insurance" hike Marvell Technology stock soars as Nvidia CEO says it's "the next trillion-dollar company" Euro area inflation continues to pick up in May, core prices nudge up as well Gold analysis shows it repaired higher. See my targets. UK mortgage approvals climbs in April, holds above six-month average European Parliament votes to remove import duties on US goods to comply with trade deal BoJ taper debate heats up as rising yields complicate exit strategy The Japanese yen extends losses as June rate hike bets continue to look wrong Palladium is looking good for the bulls. See where PA can go & where that might change Gold nudges back up today but still caught in a bit of a technical bind What are the main events for today? Iran reportedly still discussing final text of agreement, no response sent to US yet FX option expiries for 2 June 10am New York cut OpenAI Florida Lawsuit: What AI Stock Investors Should Watch Next Markets continue to stay on edge awaiting US-Iran deal Crude oil analysis today: oil futures fail near 94.20 resistance, testing lower value It's been a pretty calm session with limited data and news releases. The main highlight of the session was the release of the Eurozone Flash CPI report for May. The headline inflation rate rose to 3.2% Y/Y, matching expectations and accelerating from 3.0% in April. The increase was driven mainly by higher energy prices, while underlying inflation pressures also strengthened as Core CPI Y/Y climbed to 2.5% from 2.2% in the prior month. The data reinforced expectations that the ECB is going to raise interest rates at its June meeting but didn't change much in terms of total 2026 pricing. ECB policymaker Rehn confirmed that an interest rate increase at the June meeting should be viewed as an "insurance" move to guard against future inflation risks, even if current inflation expectations remain well anchored. The characterization of a June rate move as an "insurance hike" indicates that policymakers are unlikely to deliver a back-to-back rate hike in July as some analysts have been suggesting without a strong signal from the data. The ECB is most likely to frame the move as an insurance and then wait at least until September to see how the data and the US-Iran situation evolves over the summer. In terms of US-Iran news, Iran is reportedly still reviewing and discussing the final text of a proposed agreement with the United States and has not yet submitted an official response to Washington. While negotiations appear to be progressing, Tehran is seeking amendments and additional assurances before signing off, underscoring that significant details remain unresolved and that a final deal may not be as close as previously expected. Trump said that a deal could happen over the next week but we've heard this so many times before already. In the markets, optimism continues to prevail despite the extended US-Iran stalemate. Markets freaked out for a moment yesterday when Tasnim reported that Iran would stop message exchange with the US in protest against Israeli strikes on Lebanon but things got back to normal very quickly once Trump announced on Truth Social that there would be no troops going to Beirut, and that he had a very good call with Hezbollah which agreed that all shooting will stop. In the American session, the only highlight is the US Job Openings data for April. Job Openings are expected at 6.880M vs 8.866M prior. The data won't change anything for the Fed as all the more timely US jobs data has been pointing to a resilient/strengthening labour market. The market is pricing 15 bps of tightening for the Fed by year-end, with 47% chance of a rate hike in December. This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.

items.title: Euro area inflation continues to pick up in May, core prices nudge up as well
items.summary: CPI +3.2% vs +3.2% y/y expected Prior +3.0% Core CPI +2.5% vs +2.4% y/y expected Prior +2.2% The headline estimate moves up in May, as expected, with energy price inflation being the standout once again. Even though energy prices were down 1.1% on the month, it is still up by 10.9% year-on-year (up from 10.8% in April). Besides that, the monthly estimates show a broadening in the increase in price pressures across the region. Of note, services inflation was up 0.4% on the month. Meanwhile, inflation for non-energy industrial goods also increased by 0.2% on the month. Food price inflation was flat in May compared to the month before. Overall, services inflation jumped up to 3.5% (previously 3.0%) with food price inflation holding at 2.0%. The mix sees core annual inflation move back up to 2.5%, in a worrying sign for the ECB. If the trend continues down this path in the months ahead, rate hikes will definitely have to be put on the table as policymakers have to deal with potential spillover risks from second-round effects. As mentioned before, interest rates in Europe are now still in neutral territory. And even with 50 bps of rate hikes, it barely puts policy into being marginally restrictive. So if the ECB really wants to bring down inflation, they will have to move more aggressively. The key risk in doing that though is that policymakers risk sinking the economy further. That especially since the US-Iran conflict is already raising the stakes and inviting stagflation risks, especially in the likes of France. This article was written by Justin Low at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: CPI同比上涨3.2%，符合预期（此前为3.0%）；核心CPI同比上涨2.5%，高于预期（此前为2.2%）。5月整体CPI涨幅如预期般上升，能源价格通胀再次成为主要推手。尽管能源价格环比下降1.1%，但同比仍上涨10.9%（高于4月的10.8%）。此外，月度数据表明该地区的价格上涨压力正在扩大。值得注意的是，服务业通胀环比上涨0.4%。与此同时，非能源工业品通胀率当月也上升了0.2%。5月食品价格通胀率与上月持平。总体而言，服务业通胀率跃升至3.5%（此前为3.0%），食品价格通胀率维持在2.0%。综合来看，核心年通胀率回升至2.5%，这对欧洲央行而言是一个令人担忧的信号。如果未来几个月这一趋势持续下去，加息势必将成为政策制定者必须考虑的选项，因为他们需要应对第二轮效应带来的潜在溢出风险。如前所述，欧洲目前的利率水平仍处于中性区间。 即便加息50个基点，政策也仅能勉强达到略微收紧的程度。因此，如果欧洲央行真的想抑制通胀，就必须采取更激进的措施。但这样做的主要风险在于，决策者可能会进一步拖累经济。尤其是考虑到美伊冲突已经加剧了局势，并带来了滞胀风险，在法国等国家尤为明显。本文作者为investinglive.com的贾斯汀·洛（Justin Low）。

Even though energy prices were down 1.1% on the month, it is still up by 10.9% year-on-year (up from 10.8% in April). Besides that, the monthly estimates show a broadening in the increase in price pressures across the region. Of note, services inflation was up 0.4% on the month. Meanwhile, inflation for non-energy industrial goods also increased by 0.2% on the month. Food price inflation was flat in May compared to the month before. Overall, services inflation jumped up to 3.5% (previously 3.0%) with food price inflation holding at 2.0%. The mix sees core annual inflation move back up to 2.5%, in a worrying sign for the ECB. If the trend continues down this path in the months ahead, rate hikes will definitely have to be put on the table as policymakers have to deal with potential spillover risks from second-round effects. As mentioned before, interest rates in Europe are now still in neutral territory. And even with 50 bps of rate hikes, it barely puts policy into being marginally restrictive. So if the ECB really wants to bring down inflation, they will have to move more aggressively. The key risk in doing that though is that policymakers risk sinking the economy further. That especially since the US-Iran conflict is already raising the stakes and inviting stagflation risks, especially in the likes of France. This article was written by Justin Low at investinglive.com.
items.impactZh: 关键宏观数据超预期时，点差与滑点风险通常放大，建议降低仓位并控制杠杆。
items.impactEn: When macro data surprises, spread/slippage risk often widens; consider smaller position size and tighter leverage control.
